privileged_info_summary: Harris County, Texas contains 380 EPA TRI reporting facilities. St. James Parish, Louisiana (part of 'Cancer Alley' with 11 TRI facilities) is a known petrochemical industrial area. All 19 chemicals in the metadata valid_chemicals list (Benzene, Ethylene, Formaldehyde, Toluene, Xylene, Styrene, Butadiene, Ammonia, Hydrogen cyanide, Sulfuric acid, Hydrochloric acid, Methanol, Lead, Mercury, Dioxin, Nitrate, Zinc, Copper, Manganese) are confirmed as valid EPA TRI-listed chemicals. Real EPA TRI data shows: Harris County top chemicals include Ethylene (17% of air releases), Ammonia (9% air, 1% water), Toluene (6% air), and Nitrate compounds (96% of water releases). St. James Parish top chemicals include Ammonia (31% air, 67% water), Sulfuric acid (30% air), Methanol (16% air), Nitrate compounds (27% water), and Zinc compounds (4% water). Both locations are publicly documented in EPA TRI databases with official factsheets available.
